story_title: Add codex framework to provisioner, wizard, and config

questions.question: Do all existing tests pass after adding codex integration?
questions.actionable_check: Run `uv run pytest tests/ -v` and verify 872 tests pass with no new failures
questions.pass_criteria: All 872 tests pass with no regressions from baseline

questions.question: Are the Ansible role files created following the playwright npm pattern?
questions.actionable_check: Verify codex-ubuntu/tasks/main.yml and codex-alpine/tasks/main.yml exist, use `npm install -g @openai/codex`, and include version verification with debug output
evidence_required: task content showing npm install command
questions.pass_criteria: Both role files exist with proper npm installation pattern matching playwright roles

questions.question: Is codex added to _ROLES_WITH_VARIANTS in provisioner.py?
questions.actionable_check: Search provisioner.py for _ROLES_WITH_VARIANTS frozenset and verify 'codex' is in the list under framework roles section

questions.question: Does the provisioner's _get_base_roles() method include codex role and auto-add node when codex is in frameworks?
questions.actionable_check: Read _get_base_roles() method in provisioner.py, verify codex block exists that checks config.frameworks, auto-adds node if missing, and appends 'codex' role
questions.pass_criteria: Method includes codex with node auto-dependency following playwright pattern

questions.question: Is codex always included in frameworks list by wizard.py?
questions.actionable_check: Search wizard.py for frameworks list construction (lines ~209 and ~369), verify both locations include 'codex' alongside 'claude-code'
questions.pass_criteria: Both wizard functions include codex in default frameworks list

questions.question: Does wizard_integration.py include codex in all three locations (static defaults, post-processing, merge)?
questions.actionable_check: Check wizard_integration.py line ~86 (static defaults), lines ~185-186 (post-wizard), and lines ~543-549 (merge function) for codex inclusion
evidence_required: lines 185-186 showing codex in list construction
merge function showing codex insertion logic
questions.pass_criteria: All three locations include codex handling

questions.question: Does cli_integration.py include codex in create_wizard_defaults() for both success and fallback paths?
questions.actionable_check: Read create_wizard_defaults() in cli_integration.py, verify codex is added after frameworks extraction (line ~322) and in fallback dict (line ~348)
evidence_required: line showing codex insertion in main path
fallback dict showing codex in frameworks
questions.pass_criteria: Both paths (success and error fallback) include codex in frameworks

questions.question: Are there unit tests covering codex inclusion in provisioner's _get_base_roles()?
questions.actionable_check: Search test_provisioner.py for tests verifying codex role inclusion and node auto-dependency behavior
questions.pass_criteria: Tests exist and pass for codex framework inclusion and node dependency

mandatory_questions.question: Is there any dead code introduced by this story?
mandatory_questions.actionable_check: Search for unused functions, classes, imports in provisioner.py, wizard.py, wizard_integration.py, cli_integration.py, and new ansible role files
mandatory_questions.pass_criteria: No unused code in story scope

mandatory_questions.question: Are there any duplicate implementations?
mandatory_questions.actionable_check: Check for similar codex insertion logic patterns across wizard.py, wizard_integration.py, cli_integration.py - verify they follow consistent pattern without unnecessary duplication
mandatory_questions.pass_criteria: Codex insertion follows consistent pattern across all files without unnecessary duplication

mandatory_questions.question: Do integration tests validate the story's workflow?
mandatory_questions.actionable_check: Verify unit tests exist in test_provisioner.py and test_wizard.py covering codex inclusion behavior end-to-end
mandatory_questions.pass_criteria: Tests cover provisioner role inclusion, wizard defaults, and config merging with codex

mandatory_questions.question: Does every item in scope.includes have corresponding implementation AND verification?
mandatory_questions.actionable_check: For each scope item (Ansible roles creation, provisioner _ROLES_WITH_VARIANTS, _get_base_roles codex handling, wizard.py updates, wizard_integration.py updates, cli_integration.py updates), verify implementation exists and has a VQ
mandatory_questions.pass_criteria: Every scope item has both implementation evidence and verification question

mandatory_questions.question: Does any code in this story contain stub markers indicating incomplete implementation?
mandatory_questions.actionable_check: Grep codex-ubuntu/tasks/main.yml, codex-alpine/tasks/main.yml, provisioner.py, wizard.py, wizard_integration.py, cli_integration.py for: TODO, FIXME, HACK, XXX, placeholder, stub, 'not yet implemented'
mandatory_questions.pass_criteria: Zero stub/placeholder markers in production code

mandatory_questions.question: Do codex integrations follow existing patterns from claude-code and playwright?
mandatory_questions.actionable_check: Compare codex Ansible roles to playwright roles (npm install pattern, version check, changed_when). Compare codex default framework handling to claude-code pattern in wizard.py and wizard_integration.py. Compare codex node auto-dependency to playwright pattern in provisioner.py
mandatory_questions.pass_criteria: All codex integrations follow established patterns from existing frameworks
